title: Deeper Roots
desc: The hunt leads to Gothos Tower, where the infestation has had time to grow.
chainDesc: Track an unknown threat across the UnderWorld alongside Chaor himself.

steps.text:
Gothos Tower. Van Bloot's territory. Chaor doesn't slow down, doesn't announce himself, doesn't ask permission. He walks through the front gate like he owns it. Technically, he does.

The UnderWorld creatures inside scatter at the sight of him. Somewhere above, you can hear Van Bloot's guards scrambling.

Your scanner is going haywire. The signal here is much stronger than Lava Pond — older, more established. Whatever this thing is, it's been growing inside Gothos Tower for a long time. The walls themselves are laced with those deep-ocean tendrils, hidden behind stone.

"It's everywhere," you tell Chaor, showing him the scan. The rendering looks like a nervous system threaded through the entire foundation of the tower.

Chaor's expression darkens. "Van Bloot has been living on top of this for who knows how long and didn't notice. Or didn't report it." His fists ignite. "Either way, I'm going to have a conversation with him later."

options.label: Map the full extent of the growth through the walls
Follow Chaor deeper into the tower

steps.text:
You descend through the tower's lower levels. The air gets colder. Wetter. That's wrong — nothing in the UnderWorld should feel like deep water.

Chaor finds it in a sub-basement that Van Bloot probably doesn't even know exists. A mass of the alien material the size of a wagon, pulsing with slow, rhythmic light. The tendrils from it run in every direction — up through the walls, down into the bedrock. Up close, you can see structures inside it. Chambers. Channels. Almost like... organs.

Your scanner catches something else. A flicker of movement in the darkest corner of the chamber. A shape. Your device tries to lock on but the signature scrambles — like it's actively resisting being scanned. Then it's gone.

"Something was here," you say.

Chaor stares at the empty corner. "I know." He turns to the mass and tears into it with both hands, ripping it apart in chunks. The bioluminescent fluid sprays across the walls and sizzles where it hits stone.

When it's done, he's breathing hard. Not from exertion.

"The Colosseum. Your scanner showed the strongest signal there. Right under the heart of the UnderWorld." He meets your eyes. "Whatever this is, it's not random. Something put these here on purpose."

Chaor turns to leave. But before you can follow, a voice echoes from the shadows above.

"Leaving so soon?"

Van Bloot descends from the upper levels, wings folded, expression calculating. He lands between you and Chaor.

"I knew about the growths, Chaor. Of course I did. I've been studying them. Harvesting the energy they produce." His eyes gleam. "Do you have any idea what these things are capable of? The power they contain?"

Chaor's fists ignite. "You've been hiding this from me."

"I've been understanding it. Something you're incapable of." Van Bloot turns to you. "You. The human with the scanner. You've mapped more of this network in three days than my researchers did in three months. I could use someone with your skills."

He steps closer, voice dropping. "Chaor will destroy what he doesn't understand. He always does. But I can show you what these things really are. Help me, and I'll give you access to scans you'd never get through brute force."

Chaor snarls. "Don't listen to him. He'd sell out the entire UnderWorld for a sliver of power."

Van Bloot smiles. "And Chaor would burn the entire UnderWorld to keep it under his heel. The question is — which approach gets you better data?"

options.label: Stand with Chaor — destroy the growths
Accept Van Bloot's offer — study the growths
